assumptions: The exposure referred to is a foreign currency exposure arising on a recognised intragroup dividend-related receivable that appears in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns IFRS hedge accounting possibilities in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39, IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting model for foreign currency monetary items and exchange differences when hedge accounting is not applied.
